questions.question: According to the Dual Process Model (Stroebe & Schut), what does healthy grieving involve?

options.text: Staying focused on the loss until acceptance is reached
Avoiding reminders of the loss to protect yourself
Oscillating between loss-oriented and restoration-oriented coping
Moving through five sequential stages in order

questions.explanation: The Dual Process Model describes healthy grieving as naturally moving back and forth between loss-oriented coping (facing the grief directly) and restoration-oriented coping (engaging with daily life). Both modes are healthy and necessary.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about the timeline of grief?

options.text: Most people should feel significantly better within six months
There is no universal timetable for grief, and feeling loss for years is not a failure
If grief lasts more than a year, professional intervention is always needed
The timeline depends primarily on how close you were to the person

questions.explanation: There is no fixed timeline for grief. Many people feel their loss in some form for years, and that is not a failure. What typically changes over time is not whether you feel the loss, but how often and how intensely the waves come.

questions.question: Grief can be a valid response to which of the following types of loss?

options.text: Only the death of a close family member
Only losses that others would consider serious
Only death-related losses
Many kinds of loss including relationships, health, home, dreams, and safety

questions.explanation: Grief is a valid response to many kinds of significant loss, including death, relationship loss, health changes, loss of home or community, loss of dreams and identity, and loss of safety and trust. All of these losses are real and can produce genuine grief.

questions.question: Why has modern grief research moved beyond the Kubler-Ross "five stages" model?

options.text: The stages were never based on any real observation
People rarely move through neat, sequential stages and may experience several emotions at once
The model only applied to terminally ill patients, not grieving people
Modern people grieve differently than people in the past

questions.explanation: Modern grief research shows that people rarely move through neat, sequential stages. You may experience several emotions at once, skip some entirely, or circle back to feelings you thought were done. The five stages model was groundbreaking for its time but is not a rigid roadmap.

questions.question: Which statement best captures what grief IS, according to the lesson?

options.text: Grief is primarily an emotional reaction that needs to be expressed through crying
Grief is a sign of weakness that should be managed privately
Grief is a problem to solve as quickly as possible
Grief is a whole-person response to loss — physical, emotional, cognitive, social, and sometimes spiritual

questions.explanation: Grief is your whole-person response to loss. It is not just an emotion — it is a physical, emotional, cognitive, social, and sometimes spiritual experience that touches every part of your life. It is not weakness, not a problem to solve, and does not require crying to be valid.

title: Understanding Grief: There Is No Right Way - Quiz
